notes.note: The standard epistolary salutation: 'Marcus Cicero sends greetings (salutem dicit) to Curio.' Roman letter conventions placed the sender's name first, unlike modern practice.

notes.anchor: you have attained everything with the highest distinction
notes.note: The Latin dignitas — translated here as 'distinction' — was the cumulative social standing a Roman acquired through offices, connections, and public esteem; it was a quasi-legal asset that could be destroyed by disgrace or exile.

notes.anchor: whatever accessions of fortune and standing fall to you, you could not have attained them, had you not once as a boy obeyed my counsels
notes.note: Cicero had been a close mentor to Curio the Younger from early in his career, acting in a quasi-paternal role of amicitia; the claim here implies Cicero gave formative political and rhetorical guidance when Curio was still in his teens.
